title: Jinja2 Templates in Ansible
learningObjectives: Write Jinja2 templates with variable substitution
Use filters, tests, and control structures in templates
Use set_fact and register to create dynamic variables

theory.content:
Jinja2 Templating

Ansible uses Jinja2 as its templating engine. Templates allow you to generate dynamic configuration files using variables, filters, and control structures.

Template File (.j2)

# nginx.conf.j2
server {
    listen {{ nginx_port | default(80) }};
    server_name {{ ansible_hostname }};

    {% if enable_ssl %}
    listen 443 ssl;
    ssl_certificate {{ ssl_cert_path }};
    {% endif %}

    location / {
        root {{ web_root }};
        index index.html;
    }
}

Deploying a Template

- name: Deploy nginx config
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: nginx.conf.j2
    dest: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
    owner: root
    mode: '0644'
  notify: Restart nginx

Jinja2 Filters

Filters transform variable values using the pipe | operator:

  • {{ name | upper }} — convert to uppercase
  • {{ list | join(',') }} — join list items
  • {{ value | default('fallback') }} — provide default if undefined
  • {{ path | basename }} — extract filename from path
  • {{ string | regex_replace('old', 'new') }} — regex substitution

Control Structures

{% for host in groups['webservers'] %}
upstream backend {
    server {{ hostvars[host]['ansible_host'] }}:8080;
}
{% endfor %}

{% if ansible_distribution == 'Ubuntu' %}
# Ubuntu-specific config
{% elif ansible_distribution == 'CentOS' %}
# CentOS-specific config
{% endif %}

register and set_fact

- name: Check nginx status
  ansible.builtin.command: nginx -t
  register: nginx_test
  ignore_errors: true

- name: Set fact based on result
  ansible.builtin.set_fact:
    nginx_config_valid: "{{ nginx_test.rc == 0 }}"

- name: Show result
  ansible.builtin.debug:
    msg: "Config valid: {{ nginx_config_valid }}"
